whatItDoes: Pretty-print and view YAML with syntax highlighting. Features color-coded keys, strings, numbers, booleans, and comments. Includes validation for common YAML syntax errors, line numbers, and copy functionality for the formatted output.
whyItExists: YAML files can be difficult to read and debug without proper formatting; this tool provides instant syntax highlighting and validation, making it easy to spot errors and understand YAML structure visually.
whoShouldUse: DevOps engineers, developers working with Kubernetes/Docker configurations, CI/CD pipeline authors, and anyone who regularly reads or writes YAML files.
useCases: Quickly visualize and validate a YAML configuration file
Debug indentation errors in Kubernetes or Docker Compose YAML
Copy formatted YAML after making edits to a configuration
Understand the structure of a complex YAML document with syntax highlighting
Verify YAML syntax before committing configuration changes
Read and understand YAML files from other team members
instructions: Paste your YAML text into the input textarea
The syntax-highlighted output appears below in real-time
Color coding: blue for keys, green for strings, orange for numbers, purple for booleans
Click the Copy button to copy the formatted YAML to your clipboard
Enable Word Wrap for long lines that would otherwise require horizontal scrolling
Syntax errors are displayed in a red warning banner above the output
examples: A Kubernetes deployment YAML with pods, services, and configmaps
A Docker Compose file with multiple services and volumes
A GitHub Actions workflow with jobs, steps, and environment variables
bestPractices: Use 2 spaces for indentation consistently throughout your YAML files
Wrap string values in quotes when they contain special characters
Use --- to separate multiple YAML documents in a single file
Keep line length under 80 characters for better readability
Use comments (#) to explain complex configurations
commonMistakes: Using tabs instead of spaces for indentation — YAML requires spaces
Mixing indentation levels inconsistently within the same document
Forgetting to quote strings that contain colons, brackets, or other special characters
Using the wrong indentation depth for nested objects
Not escaping special characters in string values
faq: Why doesn't my YAML parse correctly? — The most common cause is inconsistent indentation. YAML is indentation-sensitive and requires spaces (not tabs).
What YAML features are supported? — This tool supports basic YAML: scalars, sequences, mappings, and inline collections. Advanced features like anchors and complex keys may not render correctly.
Can I edit the highlighted output? — The output is read-only for viewing. Edit the input textarea to make changes.
Is this tool suitable for large YAML files? — It works well for files up to a few hundred lines. For very large files, consider using a dedicated YAML editor.
